issueDetails.example:
"The endpoint appears to be vulnerable to Server Side Request Forgery attack. The original request was replayed by replacing CSV upload parameter with url that redirects to AWS IMDS endpoint. The application responded with 2XX success code and also gave out sensitive AWS information in response. Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) flaws occur whenever an API is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL. It allows an attacker to coerce the application to send a crafted request to an unexpected destination, even when protected by a firewall or a VPN.
 "Modern concepts in application development make SSRF more common and more dangerous.
" "More common - the following concepts encourage developers to access an external resource based on user input: Webhooks, file fetching from URLs, custom SSO, and URL previews.
" "More dangerous - Modern technologies like cloud providers, Kubernetes, and Docker expose management and control channels over HTTP on predictable, well-known paths. Those channels are an easy target for an SSRF attack.
" "It is also more challenging to limit outbound traffic from your application, because of the connected nature of modern applications.
" + "The SSRF risk can not always be completely eliminated. While choosing a protection mechanism, it is important to consider the business risks and needs."

issueDescription.example: Quite often, APIs do not impose any restrictions on the size or number of resources that can be requested by the user.

testName.example: BFLA by HTTP DELETE method overriding

testDescription.example: An attacker can construct a URL within the application that causes a redirection to an arbitrary external domain.

testDetails.example: Open redirection vulnerabilities arise when an application incorporates user-controllable data into the target of a redirection in an unsafe way.

testImpact.example: This behavior can be leveraged to facilitate phishing attacks against users of the application.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getTeamData.description: Fetch all users and pending invitations in the account

schema.description: List of users and pending invitations

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/inviteUsers.description: Send an invitation to a new user with a specific role. Validates email domain and role hierarchy before creating invite.

inviteeEmail.description: Email address of the user to invite. Must be valid email format and belong to same organization domain

inviteeName.description: Name of the user being invited

inviteeRole.description: Role to assign to invited user. Valid values: ADMIN, MEMBER, DEVELOPER, VIEWER, or custom role names. Must be within current user's role hierarchy

websiteHostName.description: Website host name for the invitation link

schema.description: User invitation request with email and role

finalInviteCode.description: Complete invitation link to be sent to the invitee. Contains JWT code valid for 1 week

inviteeRole.description: Role assigned to the invited user

schema.description: Successful invitation response containing the invite link and role

error.description: Error message. Possible values: 'Invalid email', 'Email must belong to same organisation', 'User already exists', 'Invalid role', 'User not allowed to invite for this role', 'Inviting new users is not allowed for this account'

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/removeUser.description: Remove an existing user from the account, or delete a pending invitation if user hasn't accepted yet

email.description: Email address of the user to remove from the account

200.description: User removed or invitation deleted successfully

error.description: Error message. Possible values: 'User not found' (if user doesn't exist in account and no pending invitation)

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

post.summary: Fetch OpenAPI schema for a collection
post.description:
Returns the stored OpenAPI/Swagger schema (gunzipped from the database) for the given API collection as a JSON string.

Source: `OpenApiAction.fetchOpenApiSchema()`

apiCollectionId.description: Numeric ID of the API collection whose OpenAPI schema to retrieve.

openApiSchema.description: The OpenAPI 3.x / Swagger 2.x schema as a JSON string.

post.description:
Returns the import status and per-endpoint parsing details for a previous Swagger/OpenAPI file upload identified by `uploadId`. Use this to inspect which endpoints parsed successfully, which had non-fatal errors, and which could not be imported at all.

Source: `OpenApiAction.fetchImportLogs()`

uploadId.description: ID of the Swagger file upload record to inspect.

fetch_logs.summary: Fetch import logs for an upload

uploadDetails.description: Aggregated import details for the upload.

uploadStatus.description: Overall upload status (e.g. SUCCEEDED, FAILED, PROCESSING).

collectionErrors.description: Collection-level errors that apply to the whole upload.

apisWithErrorsAndParsed.description: Endpoints with non-fatal errors that were still partially parsed.

apisWithErrorsAndCannotBeImported.description: Endpoints with fatal parse errors that cannot be imported.

correctlyParsedApis.description: Endpoints that parsed without any errors.

totalCount.description: Total number of endpoints in the upload.

post.summary: Approve and ingest a Swagger file upload
post.description:
Pushes approved records from the specified Swagger/OpenAPI upload to Kafka (topic `akto.api.logs`) for async ingestion into the Akto API inventory. Only endpoints matching the chosen `importType` are included.

Source: `OpenApiAction.importFile()`

uploadId.description: ID of the Swagger file upload to ingest.

importType.description: Whether to ingest only successfully parsed endpoints (`ONLY_SUCCESSFUL_APIS`) or all endpoints including those with non-fatal parse errors (`ALL_APIS`).

all_apis.summary: Ingest all endpoints including those with non-fatal parse errors

200.description: Import queued successfully. Ingestion happens asynchronously via Kafka.

400.description: `uploadId` is not found or records cannot be read.

post.description:
Parses a raw Imperva JSON string (max 10 MB), creates or finds an API collection named after the extracted hostname, and pushes the parsed traffic asynchronously to Kafka for ingestion into the API inventory. `generateMultipleSamples` controls whether multiple sample request/response pairs are generated per endpoint.

Source: `ImpervaImportAction.execute()`

impervaString.description: Raw Imperva JSON string. Maximum size is 10 MB.

generateMultipleSamples.description: If `true`, generates multiple sample messages per endpoint instead of just one.

basic_import.summary: Import an Imperva schema with single samples

message.example: Imperva schema imported successfully. Collection: api.example.com

400.description: `impervaString` is null/empty or exceeds the 10 MB size limit.

post.description:
Creates an API collection named after the URL's hostname (tagged as GenAI), then fires an HTTP request to the given URL asynchronously and ingests the response via Kafka. Omit `requestBody` to use GET; provide it to send a POST with that body. An optional `testRoleId` enables authenticated fetching using a pre-configured test role.

Source: `AgentImportAction.importFromUrl()`

url.description: Full URL to fetch. The hostname is used as the collection name.

testRoleId.description: Optional ObjectId hex of the test role to use for authentication when fetching the URL.

requestBody.description: Optional POST body. If set, the URL is fetched with HTTP POST using this as the body. Omit to use GET.

authenticated_get.summary: Import via GET with test role auth

post_request.summary: Import via POST with a body

post.summary: Add or update Postman API key
post.description:
Upserts the Postman credential (API key and workspace ID) for the current account in `third_party_access`. Used by subsequent Postman integration calls (`createPostmanApi`, `savePostmanCollection`).

Source: `PostmanAction.addOrUpdateApiKey()`

Builds an OpenAPI spec from the stored sample data for the given collection and pushes it to the Postman API asynchronously using the API key stored via `addOrUpdatePostmanCred`. An optional `apiInfoKeyList` restricts the export to a specific subset of endpoints; if omitted, the entire collection is exported.

Source: `PostmanAction.createPostmanApi()`

apiCollectionId.description: ID of the API collection to export.

apiInfoKeyList.description: Optional list of specific endpoints to export. If omitted the entire collection is exported.

200.description: Export queued successfully. Upload to Postman happens asynchronously.

post.summary: Import a Postman collection into Akto
post.description:
Fetches the specified Postman collection via the stored Postman API key, converts it through Akto's Node.js conversion service, and stores the resulting APISpec document in MongoDB.

Source: `PostmanAction.savePostmanCollection()`

postmanCollectionId.description: Postman collection UUID (found in the Postman share URL).

apiCollectionId.description: Akto collection ID to associate with the imported Postman collection.

post.summary: Approve and ingest a Postman file upload
post.description:
Pushes approved records from the specified Postman file upload to Kafka (topic `akto.api.logs`) for async ingestion. Only endpoints matching the chosen `importType` are included.

Source: `PostmanAction.importFile()`

uploadId.description: ID of the Postman file upload to ingest.

importType.description: Whether to ingest only successfully parsed endpoints or all endpoints including those with non-fatal parse errors.

post.description:
Returns import status and per-endpoint parsing details for a previous Postman file upload.

Source: `PostmanAction.fetchImportLogs()`

uploadId.description: ID of the Postman file upload record to inspect.

fetch_logs.summary: Fetch import logs for a Postman upload

post.summary: Mark a Postman upload for deletion
post.description:
Sets `markedForDeletion: true` on the specified upload record so it is excluded from future import log listings.

Source: `PostmanAction.markImportForDeletion()`

uploadId.description: ID of the Postman upload to mark for deletion.

post.summary: Convert sample data to a Burp Suite request
post.description:
Converts a single Akto HTTP sample message (a JSON string encoding the full request + response) into a raw HTTP request string compatible with Burp Suite. The returned string uses the URL path (not the full absolute URL) and omits the `Content-Length` header.

Note: the endpoint name contains a typo (`Samle`) which is preserved for backwards compatibility.

Source: `ExportSampleDataAction.generateBurpRequest()`

sampleData.description: Akto HTTP sample message JSON string encoding both the request and response.

burpRequest.description: Raw HTTP request in Burp Suite format using the URL path.
burpRequest.example:
GET /api/users HTTP/1.1
Host: api.example.com
Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_JWT]

post.summary: Bulk-export collection sample data for Burp Suite
post.description:
Paginates through the sample data of the named collection and returns Burp Suite-compatible raw HTTP request + response strings for each endpoint. Pagination is cursor-based: pass `lastUrlFetched` and `lastMethodFetched` from the previous response to fetch the next page. Maximum 500 endpoints per page.

Source: `ExportSampleDataAction.importInBurp()`

collectionName.description: Name of the API collection to paginate.

lastUrlFetched.description: Pagination cursor: URL returned at the end of the previous page. Omit on the first request.

lastMethodFetched.description: Pagination cursor: HTTP method returned at the end of the previous page. Omit on the first request.

limit.description: Number of endpoints per page. Values ≤ 0 or > 500 are clamped to 500.

next_page.summary: Next page using cursor from previous response

importInBurpResult.description: List of Burp-formatted entries for this page.

req.description: Raw HTTP request in Burp format (with absolute URL).
req.example:
GET https://api.example.com/api/users HTTP/1.1
Host: api.example.com
Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_JWT]

res.description: Raw HTTP response in Burp format.

lastUrlFetched.description: Pass this value as `lastUrlFetched` in the next request to get the next page. `null` when there is no more data.

lastMethodFetched.description: Pass this value as `lastMethodFetched` in the next request.

400.description: `collectionName` does not match any existing collection.

post.summary: Record a Burp plugin JAR download
post.description:
Updates the `lastDownloadedAt` timestamp for the current user and returns success. Call this endpoint when the user downloads the Akto Burp extension JAR to track adoption metrics. Returns no body fields beyond success.

Source: `BurpJarAction.execute()`

post.summary: Get the Burp plugin JAR download URL
post.description:
Returns the static GitHub raw URL for the latest Akto Burp extension JAR (`akto-burp-extension` repository, master branch).

Source: `BurpJarAction.fetchBurpPluginDownloadLink()`

burpGithubLink.description: GitHub raw URL for the Akto Burp extension JAR.

post.summary: Fetch or create Burp Suite credentials
post.description:
Finds or creates a BURP-type utility API token for the current account. The `host` in the response is derived from the HTTP `Origin` request header sent by the Burp plugin.

Source: `BurpJarAction.fetchBurpCredentials()`

apiToken.description: BURP-type utility API token for the account.

host.description: Host extracted from the `Origin` HTTP header of the request (used by the Burp plugin to identify the Akto server).

post.description:
Returns the last boot-up timestamp and version string of the Akto Burp extension as recorded by the plugin during its last startup.

Source: `BurpJarAction.fetchBurpPluginInfo()`

burpPluginInfo.description: Runtime details of the Burp plugin.

lastBootUpTimestamp.description: Unix epoch when the Burp plugin last booted.

post.description:
Stores a recorded login flow and optionally executes it to extract an auth token. Two modes are supported:

- **Standard mode** (default): `miniTestingServiceName` is null or `hybridTestingEnabled` is false for the account. The flow is triggered asynchronously. `testingRunPlaygroundId` is null in the response.
- **Hybrid mode**: `miniTestingServiceName` is set AND the account has `hybridTestingEnabled`. A `TestingRunPlayground` document is created and its ID is returned. Poll `fetchRecordedFlowOutput` with this ID to retrieve the token.

Source: `LoginRecorderAction.uploadRecordedFlow()`

content.description: JSON string representing the recorded login flow steps.

tokenFetchCommand.description: JavaScript expression or command executed after the login flow to extract the auth token (e.g. `document.cookie`).

roleName.description: Name of the test role to associate with this login flow.

miniTestingServiceName.description: If set and the account has `hybridTestingEnabled`, activates hybrid mode and a `TestingRunPlayground` ID is returned for polling.

hybrid_mode.summary: Hybrid mode — returns playground ID for polling

200.description: Flow uploaded. `testingRunPlaygroundId` is non-null only in hybrid mode.

testingRunPlaygroundId.description: ObjectId hex of the `TestingRunPlayground` document. `null` in standard mode.

post.summary: Poll for recorded login flow output
post.description:
Polls for the result of a recorded login flow execution. In hybrid mode, pass the `testingRunPlaygroundId` returned by `uploadRecordedFlow`. When the flow completes, `token` contains the extracted auth token JSON (with an `all_cookies` array). While still running, `tokenFetchInProgress` is `true` and `token` is null.

Source: `LoginRecorderAction.fetchRecordedFlowOutput()`

testingRunPlaygroundId.description: ObjectId hex for hybrid mode polling. Omit for standard (non-hybrid) mode.

hybrid_poll.summary: Poll using a playground ID (hybrid mode)

token.description: Extracted auth token JSON string (contains `all_cookies` array). `null` while the flow is still executing.

tokenFetchInProgress.description: `true` while the flow is still executing; `false` when complete.

post.description:
Returns base64-encoded PNG screenshots captured during the last login flow execution for the given test role.

Source: `LoginRecorderAction.fetchRecordedLoginScreenshots()`

roleName.description: Name of the test role whose login screenshots to fetch.

fetch_screenshots.summary: Fetch screenshots for the admin role

screenshotsBase64.description: List of base64-encoded PNG screenshots from the login flow.

screenshotsUpdatedAt.description: Unix epoch when the screenshots were last updated.

post.description:
Creates or reuses an API collection named after the target hostname (tagging it DAST), then starts a crawl run. Supports multiple authentication strategies: username/password form auth, API key, and test role-based token extraction (via recorded login flow or login step builder). Custom HTTP headers, URL template patterns for path normalisation, and seed application pages are all supported.

If `selectedModuleName` is set, the named DAST module must have sent a heartbeat within the last 5 minutes; otherwise the request returns an error. If null, falls back to the internal Akto DAST crawler service.

`crawlingTime` must be between 600 (10 minutes) and 345600 (4 days) seconds.

Source: `AktoJaxAction.initiateCrawler()`

hostname.description: Full URL of the target application (e.g. `https://app.example.com`). The hostname is used as the collection name unless `collectionName` is provided.

crawlingTime.description: Maximum crawl duration in seconds. Must be between 600 (10 min) and 345600 (4 days).

outscopeUrls.description: Comma-separated URL paths to exclude from the crawl.

testRoleHexId.description: Optional ObjectId hex of the test role to use for cookie/token extraction. Used when neither username/password nor API key is provided.

apiKey.description: Optional API key to pass to the crawler service.

dashboardUrl.description: Optional Akto dashboard URL to pass to the internal crawler service.

runTestAfterCrawling.description: If `true`, automatically triggers security tests on all discovered endpoints after the crawl completes.

selectedMiniTestingService.description: Name of the mini testing service to use for post-crawl tests. Only relevant when `runTestAfterCrawling` is `true`.

selectedModuleName.description: Name of the external DAST module to use. The module must have sent a heartbeat within the last 5 minutes. Falls back to the internal Akto DAST service when null.

collectionName.description: Custom collection name. Defaults to the extracted hostname when not provided.

customHeaders.description: Additional HTTP headers to include in every crawl request.

urlTemplatePatterns.description: Comma-separated URL template patterns used to normalise path parameters (e.g. `/users/{id},/orders/{orderId}`).

applicationPages.description: Comma-separated seed pages from which the crawl starts.

basic_crawl.summary: Minimal crawl — hostname and duration only

authenticated_crawl.summary: Crawl with test role auth and post-crawl testing

external_module.summary: Crawl using an external DAST module

200.description: Crawler initiated. A `CrawlerRun` document is created in MongoDB.

400.description: `crawlingTime` is outside the 600–345600 range, the selected DAST module is not available, or authentication token extraction via the test role failed.

post.description:
Pushes raw crawler traffic data (in Akto HTTP sample format) for the given collection to Kafka (topic `akto.api.logs`) for async ingestion.

Source: `AktoJaxAction.uploadCrawlerData()`

apiCollectionId.description: Numeric ID of the target API collection, passed as a string.

crawlerData.description: Akto HTTP sample message JSON captured by the crawler.

400.description: `apiCollectionId` does not match any known collection.

post.description:
Updates the status of an existing crawl run identified by `crawlId`. Depending on the new status:
- `RUNNING` — sets `startTimestamp` to now
- `COMPLETED` — sets `endTimestamp` to now and optionally triggers post-crawl security tests if `runTestAfterCrawling` was set when the crawl was initiated
- `FAILED` — sets `endTimestamp` to now and stores the `errorMessage`
- `STOPPED` — sets `endTimestamp` to now

Source: `AktoJaxAction.updateCrawlerStatus()`

crawlId.description: UUID of the crawl run to update.

status.description: New status to set on the crawl run.

errorMessage.description: Error details. Only stored when `status` is `FAILED`.

mark_failed.summary: Mark a crawl as failed with an error

post.description:
Sends a stop request to the internal AKTOJAX crawler service and sets the crawl run's status to `STOP_REQUESTED`. The crawler service acknowledges the stop and will complete its current work before shutting down.

Source: `AktoJaxAction.stopCrawler()`

crawlId.description: UUID of the crawl run to stop.

200.description: Stop request sent and crawl status set to `STOP_REQUESTED`.

400.description: `crawlId` is null/empty or the crawler service returned a non-success status.

post.summary: Record a URL discovered by the crawler
post.description:
Stores a single URL that was discovered during a crawl, along with provenance information (the source page, the XPath of the triggering element, and the button text) and whether it was accepted (within the crawl scope).

Source: `AktoJaxAction.saveCrawlerUrl()`

crawlId.description: UUID of the crawl run that discovered this URL.

accepted.description: Whether this URL was within the crawl scope.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch when the URL was discovered.

sourceUrl.description: Page URL on which the link to this URL was found.

sourceXpath.description: XPath of the element that triggered navigation to this URL.

buttonText.description: Text content of the element that linked to this URL.

accepted_url.summary: Save an accepted (in-scope) URL with provenance

post.description:
Returns all `CrawlerRun` documents that are in `RUNNING`, `COMPLETED`, `STOP_REQUESTED`, or `STOPPED` state, plus legacy records that have no `status` field. `PENDING` and `FAILED` runs are excluded.

Source: `DastAction.fetchAllDastScans()`

post.summary: Fetch URLs discovered in a DAST scan
post.description:
Returns all `CrawlerUrl` documents recorded by the crawler for the specified `crawlId`, representing every URL that was discovered (both in-scope and out-of-scope).

Source: `DastAction.fetchDastScan()`

crawlId.description: UUID of the crawl run whose discovered URLs to retrieve.

fetch_scan.summary: Fetch discovered URLs for a crawl

200.description: List of discovered crawler URL documents.

crawlerUrls.description: All `CrawlerUrl` documents for the given crawl.

post.description:
Returns all external DAST modules that have sent a heartbeat within the last 5 minutes. An empty `availableModules` list means only the built-in internal Akto DAST crawler is available and `selectedModuleName` should be left null when calling `initiateCrawler`.

Source: `AktoJaxAction.fetchAvailableDastModules()`

availableModules.description: DAST modules that sent a heartbeat within the last 5 minutes. Empty if only the built-in service is available.

name.description: Internal module name. Pass this as `selectedModuleName` to `initiateCrawler`.

lastHeartbeat.description: Unix epoch of the last heartbeat received from this module.

ImportLogEntry.description: A single per-endpoint parse result entry in an import log.

term.description: Method and path of the endpoint (e.g. `GET /users`).

description.description: Human-readable parse result or error message.

CrawlerRun.description: A `CrawlerRun` document from MongoDB representing one DAST crawl execution.

startedBy.description: Email of the user who initiated the crawl.

startTimestamp.description: Unix epoch when the crawl started.

endTimestamp.description: Unix epoch when the crawl ended (0 if not yet finished).

moduleName.description: Name of the DAST module used.

collectionId.description: ID of the API collection populated by this crawl.

CrawlerUrl.description: A `CrawlerUrl` document representing a single URL discovered during a DAST crawl.

crawlId.description: UUID of the crawl run that discovered this URL.

accepted.description: Whether this URL was within the crawl scope.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch when the URL was discovered.

sourceUrl.description: Page where the link to this URL was found.

sourceXpath.description: XPath of the element that triggered navigation.

buttonText.description: Text of the element that linked to this URL.

/api/fetchCodeAnalysisApiInfos.description: Fetch code-analysis API info for a given traffic collection. Returns the matched CodeAnalysisCollection record and a map of all endpoints discovered from source code, keyed by 'METHOD /path'.

200.description: Code analysis API info retrieved successfully

codeAnalysisCollection.description: Null when no source-code scan has been linked to this collection

codeAnalysisApisMap.description: Map keyed by 'METHOD /endpoint'. Each value is a CodeAnalysisApiInfo document.

discoveredTs.description: Unix epoch seconds when the endpoint was first discovered in source code

lastSeenTs.description: Unix epoch seconds of the most recent scan that found this endpoint

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/addCodeAnalysisRepo.description: Add one or more source-code repositories for code-analysis scanning. Uses upsert — re-adding an existing repo is a no-op and will not reset its lastRun or scheduleTime.

codeAnalysisRepos.description: List of repositories to register. Must not be empty.

400.description: Validation error — codeAnalysisRepos list was null or empty

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/deleteCodeAnalysisRepo.description: Delete a source-code repository registration. Removes all documents matching the given projectName and repoName combination. Does not delete the associated CodeAnalysisCollection or its API info.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchCodeAnalysisRepos.description: Fetch all registered source-code repositories, optionally filtered by source-code type. Defaults to BITBUCKET (includes repos where sourceCodeType is absent for backward compatibility).

sourceCodeType.description: Filter by SCM provider. Omit or set to BITBUCKET to also match legacy repos without a type field.

id.description: MongoDB ObjectId of the repo document

lastRun.description: Unix epoch seconds of the last completed scan. 0 if never run.

scheduleTime.description: Unix epoch seconds when the next scan is scheduled

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/runCodeAnalysisRepo.description: Trigger an immediate scan for one or more registered repositories by setting their scheduleTime to now. Only updates repos that already exist (upsert:false).

codeAnalysisRepos.description: List of repos to trigger. Only repoName and projectName are required; other fields are ignored.

200.description: Scan triggered successfully — scheduleTime updated to current timestamp

400.description: Validation error — codeAnalysisRepos list was null or empty

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getSourceCodeCollectionsForDirector.description: Given a backend directory path prefix, return all CodeAnalysisCollections that have at least one API endpoint whose source file lives under that directory. Returns endpoint counts per collection to help the caller decide which collections to load fully.

chosenBackendDirectory.description: Directory path prefix used as a regex anchor (^prefix.*) to filter CodeAnalysisApiInfo by location.filePath

name.description: Collection name (matches the corresponding API collection name)

count.description: Number of endpoints whose file path matches the given directory prefix

apiCollectionId.description: ID of the linked traffic API collection

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getApisForChosenCollectionForSource.description: Fetch request/response schema information for all API endpoints in the specified CodeAnalysisCollections. Returns a flat list of endpoints enriched with their inferred JSON schemas, suitable for displaying source-code-discovered APIs alongside their traffic data.

chosenCodeAnalysisCollectionIds.description: Hex ObjectId strings of CodeAnalysisCollections to load. Pass an empty array to return an empty result.

200.description: API endpoints with schemas retrieved successfully

apiCollectionId.description: ID of the linked traffic API collection

url.description: Endpoint URL path, possibly containing Akto template tokens for parameterised segments

requestSchema.description: Inferred JSON schema for the request body. May be empty if no request body detected.

responseSchema.description: Inferred JSON schema for the response body. May be empty if not yet analysed.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs
